frontier: Make the direction review moment feel legible, informative, and worthy of trust — the user should understand what they're approving before they approve it.
goal: Transform DirectionView from a minimal rationale dump into a clear, multi-panel brief that shows the proposed direction, what will change, and invites deliberate approval.

tasks.description: Rebuild DirectionView with richer layout: header with goal, rationale panel, direction preview panel, changeset panels for docs, and footer with approval controls
tasks.instructions:
Rewrite src/modules/main-panel/direction.view.tsx to:

1. Create a three-column or multi-section layout with clear visual hierarchy:
   - Header section: Display the direction proposal's goal (extracted from context or as a section)
   - Main section: Show the rationale with better typography and spacing (not raw <pre>)
   - Preview section: Show the proposed direction text (direction.direction) in a formatted panel
   - Changes section: List the docs/conventions that will be written (direction.docs and direction.conventions)

2. Style each section as a distinct card with:
   - Title/label (e.g., "Rationale", "Direction", "Changes")
   - Content area with proper typography (not <pre> blocks — use line-height, word-wrap, proper colors)
   - Subtle background and border to show elevation (var(--surface-raised))

3. Footer with approval controls:
   - Approve button (primary, var(--accent))
   - Discard button (secondary, red)
   - Error message if any
   - Acting state reflected in button appearance

4. Use the design language:
   - Colors from globals.css (foreground, foreground-muted, surface-raised, border)
   - Tailwind for layout, spacing, flex
   - No hardcoded colors
   - Restraint: let the content breathe, use whitespace

5. Data to display from DirectionProposal:
   - direction.rationale (main motivation for this direction)
   - direction.direction (the actual direction text to show)
   - direction.conventions (the conventions document — show that it will be written)
   - direction.docs (array of DocDiff; show each will be updated)

The goal is to make it feel like a briefing, not a raw dump. The user should see what they're approving, understand why, and know what documents will change.
